chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No.2896 of 2024 [Muhammad Abdul Rehman v. The State] ______________________________________________________________ Date Order With Signature Of Judge ______________________________________________________________ For hg of bail applications 24.12.2024. Mr. Hameer Khan Shar, advocate for applicant. Ms. Seema Zaidi, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh. ---------- MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Applicant was arrested on 31.07.2024 by Excise Police near New Sabzi Mandi, Super Highway, Karachi and from his personal search, 3400 grams Charas was recovered. Hence this FIR. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that in this case complainant has performed all the roles viz. Investigating Officer and he also took the sample for chemical examination to Lab. 3. Learned Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh has opposed bail. Prima facie, there is reasonable ground to believe that the applicant has committed the alleged offence, which is punishable up to more than ten years. No case for bail is made out. Accordingly, the bail application is dismissed. However, the trial Court is directed to examine the material witnesses within a period of four months, after which, the applicant would be at liberty to move the bail application for a fresh consideration. The Bail Application is disposed of accordingly. The observations herein above are tentative in nature and shall not prejudice the case of either party on merits before the trial Court. JUDGE HANIF
